Details:
Removal of acetochlor by biological treatment was found to be quite effective. One bench-scale study reported removal of between 84 and greater than 99 percent by mixed microbial cultures, primarily comprised of strains belonging to Pseudomonas and Bacillus. Another bench-scale study performed with biofilms colonized on polycarbonate film bands placed in surface water spiked with acetochlor was less effective at removing the herbicide, resulting in 26 percent removal.
March 2009
